headline: Refreshes the vendored framework, hardening redirect and header handling
summary: A maintenance release that refreshes the vendored cryptography and identity framework to its current release. The refresh hardens the redirect and protocol-header handling HermitStash builds on — most visibly, a redirect target carrying a horizontal tab is now refused, closing an open-redirect a browser's URL parser would otherwise collapse to an attacker origin. No configuration changes are required.

items.title: Redirect targets reject a horizontal tab (open-redirect hardening)
items.body: The framework's redirect helper — which HermitStash's sign-in, OAuth, and admin flows use — now refuses a redirect target containing a horizontal tab, alongside the carriage-return, line-feed, and NUL it already rejected. A browser's URL parser strips a tab before resolving the address, so a target such as a tab followed by an authority would otherwise collapse to a protocol-relative address and navigate off-origin, slipping past the same-origin and allowlist checks. HermitStash inherits the fix by refreshing the framework.

items.title: Refreshed the vendored cryptography and identity framework
items.body: The vendored framework was updated to its current release, picking up its latest security and correctness hardening across the redirect, protocol-header, mail-authentication, federated-identity, and keychain primitives — including several fail-open and header-injection fixes in the paths HermitStash composes.
